objective: Track deep-research gaps needed before the MLX porting skill can credibly claim comprehensive coverage.

items.summary: Golden scenarios now cover all 14 declared architecture families with reproducible synthetic fixtures.
items.required_gate: Keep `tests.test_scenarios` green at 14/14 families: each declared family must route correctly, select the expected runbook, prove source weight keys, catch a seeded parity bug, and include or exclude one relevant optimization.

items.summary: Static intake now emits ONNX and GGUF manifests, but source formats such as JAX/Flax, TensorFlow/Keras, Core ML, and checkpoint-only repos still need first-class static extraction and fixtures.
items.required_gate: Static manifest extraction without import or execution: keep ONNX ModelProto/opset/initializer/external-data inspection and GGUF header/metadata/tensor table coverage green; add Flax/Orbax tree metadata, TensorFlow SavedModel and Keras archive signatures/config, Core ML package metadata, safetensors-only hold rules, unsupported-op reports, deterministic weight-map coverage, and no silent fallback to manual routing.

items.summary: The top-1000 contributor sweep requested all ml-explore/mlx contributor pages, refreshed 256 linked contributors and 262 anon=true author buckets, and screened 71 repository matches, but GitHub code-search rate limits interrupted several long-tail queries.
items.required_gate: Refresh contributors by following GitHub Link headers until no rel="next" remains or the explicit cap is reached; record requested count, linked count, anon=true count without raw anonymous identities, status codes, ETags, Last-Modified headers, rate-limit state, repository/code search total_count and incomplete_results, replay missed queries, inspect newly matched repos by architecture/source-format family, and record each result as adopted, held, rejected, or backlog.

items.summary: Non-generative computer vision ports need first-class guidance for CNNs, ConvNeXt/EfficientNet, detection, segmentation, OCR/layout, SAM-like masks, depth, and pose.
items.required_gate: Preprocessing parity, NCHW/NHWC conversion, logits/masks/boxes parity, coordinate and resize semantics, IoU/mAP/top-k gates, and readable MLX or NumPy fallbacks for NMS, ROIAlign, and grid-sample-like paths.

items.summary: Structured, tabular, time-series, recommender, and ranking models are not first-class porting families.
items.required_gate: Scaler/normalizer parity, lag construction, observed-mask handling, context/prediction split, known-future covariate leakage checks, categorical vocab and missing-value policy, jagged offsets/lengths and embedding pooling parity, candidate-id versus score-based retrieval checks, NDCG/AUC/top-k/forecast-error task gates.

items.summary: GNN, point-cloud, protein, equivariant, simulator, and chemistry ports are absent from the current first-class coverage map.
items.required_gate: Scatter/segment/reduce parity, permutation invariance, rotation/reflection equivariance probes, neighbor-list/radius graph fixtures, ragged batching, sparse/dense aggregation comparisons, and units/symmetry/energy-force constraints.

items.summary: Training and fine-tuning guidance should become a full port target with adapter, distillation, checkpointing, optimizer-state, resume, and trainable custom-kernel tracks.
items.required_gate: Tiny overfit, loss/gradient parity, optimizer state round-trip, checkpoint resume, deterministic seed, and memory graph-retention check.
